<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yoshiwara Gion-sai]][[ja:吉原祇園祭]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11412729]] Wikidata name: Yoshiwara Gion-sai [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox_holiday | Event Name = Yoshiwara Gion Festival | English Title = Yoshiwara Gion-sai | image = | image Size = | image Description = | Type = {{ill|Festival|ja|祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭|1=festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132241}} | Nickname = Oten no San | Official Name = | Former Name = | Date = 2nd Saturday and Sunday of June | Last Held = 2022 | Time = 15:00 - 21:00 | Venue = Around the Yoshiwara Shopping Street | Organized By = Yoshiwara Gion Festival Executive Committee | Co-organizers = | Sponsors = | Support = | Cooperation = | Management = | Access Name = | Nearest Station = {{ill|Gakunan Railway|ja|岳南鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakunan Railway|1=Gakunan Railway Company Ltd.|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24886051}} [[:en:Yoshiwara-honchō Station|Yoshiwara Honmachi Station]] | Direct Bus = | Parking = | URL = http://www.yoshiwara.net/gion/ | Notes = }}
'''Yoshiwara Gion Festival''' (吉原祇園祭, Yoshiwara Gion-sai) is a festival held annually on the second Saturday and Sunday of June in the Yoshiwara district of [[:en:Fuji, Shizuoka|Fuji, Shizuoka]], formerly [[:en:Yoshiwara, Shizuoka|Yoshiwara City]], Shizuoka Prefecture.
== Overview == * One of the historically long-standing festivals originating during the time of Yoshiwara City. * Known locally as "Oten no San," it is called the foremost Gion Festival in the Tōkai region. * The primary venue, {{ill|Yoshiwara Shopping Street|ja|吉原商店街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Shopping Street|1=Yoshiwara Shopping Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133934514}}, stretches approximately 1 km with over 200 stalls lining the streets, attracting more than 200,000 visitors over two days. * Although named a [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]], it is not a direct continuation of Kyoto's Gion Festival. The style of the festival reflects influences from various local traditions, as Yoshiwara was a {{ill|Shukuba|lt=Shukuba|lt=Post town|ja|宿場町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/post town|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宿場}}.
== Venue == * Centered around {{ill|Yoshiwara Shopping Street|ja|吉原商店街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Shopping Street|1=Yoshiwara Shopping Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133934514}} in the former Yoshiwara city center (now the central area of Fuji City). * From 13:00 to 21:00 during the festival, the shopping street is closed to vehicular traffic. * Access: ** Train: Alight at [[:en:Yoshiwara-honchō Station|Yoshiwara Honmachi Station]] on the {{ill|Gakunan Railway Line|ja|岳南電車岳南線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakunan Railway Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岳南電車岳南鉄道線|1=Gakunan Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5517715}}. ** Bus: Alight at {{ill|Yoshiwara Chūō Station|ja|吉原中央駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Chūō Station|1=Yoshiwara Chūō Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412690}} on {{ill|Fujikyu Shizuoka Bus|ja|富士急静岡バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujikyu Shizuoka Bus|1=Fujikyu Shizuoka Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11456063}}.
== Floats == [[File:Dashi.jpg|thumb|300px|Parading floats]] * Reflecting the cultural crossroads of the Tōkaidō, the festival features three types of floats: "traditional float," "stage float," and a hybrid combining elements of both. These 21 unique floats parade through the town. * The tradition reportedly began in the third year of Manji (1660) when a shopkeeper, having prayed to the deity [[:en:Tenjin|Tenjin]], fulfilled a vow by donating a shrine. During the year's Tenjin festival, residents mounted wheels on boats and pulled them in a procession.
== Mikoshi (Portable Shrine) == * The [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]], representing {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオノミコト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, is known for its fierce and dynamic style, earning the nickname "Kenka Mikoshi" (Fighting Shrine). * In the past, the mikoshi was handled so violently that it occasionally damaged houses. Locals refer to carrying the mikoshi as "shaking" rather than carrying, reflecting its vigorous movement. * Bamboo leaves attached to the mikoshi are considered talismans for warding off evil when displayed at home.<ref group="Note">This belief originates from the {{ill|Somin Shōrai legend|ja|蘇民将来伝説|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Somin Shōrai legend|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which says Susanoo will protect homes marked with bamboo leaves.</ref>
== Taiko Drumming == [[File:Miyadaiko.jpg|thumb|300px|Shrine Drums]] * Performed on {{ill|Nagadō Taiko|ja|長胴太鼓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagadō Taiko|1=nagadō daiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653585}} by two drummers, originally used to alert the community to the mikoshi's arrival. Known as "Call Drums" or "Fure Drums." * On Saturday evening, representatives from various neighborhoods compete in performances across Yoshiwara Shopping Street. * On Sunday, the drums fulfill their traditional role as "Call Drums" preceding the mikoshi.
== Oten no San Stamp Rally == * Visitors collect stamps at neighborhood venues marked in the festival guide. Collecting at least 10 stamps qualifies for a prize draw at Lacrosse Yoshiwara 2F Community F. * Stamp collection: Sunday of the festival, 13:00–15:00. * Prize draw: Lacrosse Yoshiwara 2F, Sunday, 13:00–15:00.
== Additional Notes == * Due to the [[:en:Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]], the festival in 2011 was limited to mikoshi rituals, with taiko performances and food stalls canceled. * In 2023, to prevent the involvement of {{ill|yakuza|ja|暴力団|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yakuza|1=Japanese organized crime group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11090465}}, stall owners and workers were required to display photo IDs.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/20230611-OYT1T50131/2/ |title=Preventing Organized Crime at Festivals: Yomiuri Shimbun |publisher=Yomiuri Shimbun |date=June 11, 2023 |accessdate=June 12, 2023}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == See Also == * [[:en:Yoshiwara-juku|Yoshiwara-juku]] * {{ill|Yoshiwara Shopping Street|ja|吉原商店街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Shopping Street|1=Yoshiwara Shopping Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133934514}} * [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]]
== External Links == * [http://www.yoshiwara.net/gion/ Yoshiwara Gion Festival] * [http://yoshiwara-shoutengai.com/ Yoshiwara Shopping Street Promotion Association] * [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=X8yxreVhew8&feature=youtu.be Yoshiwara Gion Festival Introduction Video by Fuji City] {{Event-stub}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yoshiwara Gion-sai]][[ja:吉原祇園祭]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11412729]] Wikidata name: Yoshiwara Gion-sai [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox_holiday | Event Name = Yoshiwara Gion Festival | English Title = Yoshiwara Gion-sai | image = | image Size = | image Description = | Type = {{ill|Festival|ja|祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭|1=festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132241}} | Nickname = Oten no San | Official Name = | Former Name = | Date = 2nd Saturday and Sunday of June | Last Held = 2022 | Time = 15:00 - 21:00 | Venue = Around the Yoshiwara Shopping Street | Organized By = Yoshiwara Gion Festival Executive Committee | Co-organizers = | Sponsors = | Support = | Cooperation = | Management = | Access Name = | Nearest Station = {{ill|Gakunan Railway|ja|岳南鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakunan Railway|1=Gakunan Railway Company Ltd.|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24886051}} [[:en:Yoshiwara-honchō Station|Yoshiwara Honmachi Station]] | Direct Bus = | Parking = | URL = http://www.yoshiwara.net/gion/ | Notes = }}
'''Yoshiwara Gion Festival''' (吉原祇園祭, Yoshiwara Gion-sai) is a festival held annually on the second Saturday and Sunday of June in the Yoshiwara district of [[:en:Fuji, Shizuoka|Fuji, Shizuoka]], formerly [[:en:Yoshiwara, Shizuoka|Yoshiwara City]], Shizuoka Prefecture.
== Overview == * One of the historically long-standing festivals originating during the time of Yoshiwara City. * Known locally as "Oten no San," it is called the foremost Gion Festival in the Tōkai region. * The primary venue, {{ill|Yoshiwara Shopping Street|ja|吉原商店街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Shopping Street|1=Yoshiwara Shopping Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133934514}}, stretches approximately 1 km with over 200 stalls lining the streets, attracting more than 200,000 visitors over two days. * Although named a [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]], it is not a direct continuation of Kyoto's Gion Festival. The style of the festival reflects influences from various local traditions, as Yoshiwara was a {{ill|Shukuba|lt=Shukuba|lt=Post town|ja|宿場町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/post town|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宿場}}.
== Venue == * Centered around {{ill|Yoshiwara Shopping Street|ja|吉原商店街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Shopping Street|1=Yoshiwara Shopping Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133934514}} in the former Yoshiwara city center (now the central area of Fuji City). * From 13:00 to 21:00 during the festival, the shopping street is closed to vehicular traffic. * Access: ** Train: Alight at [[:en:Yoshiwara-honchō Station|Yoshiwara Honmachi Station]] on the {{ill|Gakunan Railway Line|ja|岳南電車岳南線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakunan Railway Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岳南電車岳南鉄道線|1=Gakunan Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5517715}}. ** Bus: Alight at {{ill|Yoshiwara Chūō Station|ja|吉原中央駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Chūō Station|1=Yoshiwara Chūō Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412690}} on {{ill|Fujikyu Shizuoka Bus|ja|富士急静岡バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujikyu Shizuoka Bus|1=Fujikyu Shizuoka Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11456063}}.
== Floats == [[File:Dashi.jpg|thumb|300px|Parading floats]] * Reflecting the cultural crossroads of the Tōkaidō, the festival features three types of floats: "traditional float," "stage float," and a hybrid combining elements of both. These 21 unique floats parade through the town. * The tradition reportedly began in the third year of Manji (1660) when a shopkeeper, having prayed to the deity [[:en:Tenjin|Tenjin]], fulfilled a vow by donating a shrine. During the year's Tenjin festival, residents mounted wheels on boats and pulled them in a procession.
== Mikoshi (Portable Shrine) == * The [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]], representing {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオノミコト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, is known for its fierce and dynamic style, earning the nickname "Kenka Mikoshi" (Fighting Shrine). * In the past, the mikoshi was handled so violently that it occasionally damaged houses. Locals refer to carrying the mikoshi as "shaking" rather than carrying, reflecting its vigorous movement. * Bamboo leaves attached to the mikoshi are considered talismans for warding off evil when displayed at home.<ref group="Note">This belief originates from the {{ill|Somin Shōrai legend|ja|蘇民将来伝説|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Somin Shōrai legend|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which says Susanoo will protect homes marked with bamboo leaves.</ref>
== Taiko Drumming == [[File:Miyadaiko.jpg|thumb|300px|Shrine Drums]] * Performed on {{ill|Nagadō Taiko|ja|長胴太鼓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagadō Taiko|1=nagadō daiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653585}} by two drummers, originally used to alert the community to the mikoshi's arrival. Known as "Call Drums" or "Fure Drums." * On Saturday evening, representatives from various neighborhoods compete in performances across Yoshiwara Shopping Street. * On Sunday, the drums fulfill their traditional role as "Call Drums" preceding the mikoshi.
== Oten no San Stamp Rally == * Visitors collect stamps at neighborhood venues marked in the festival guide. Collecting at least 10 stamps qualifies for a prize draw at Lacrosse Yoshiwara 2F Community F. * Stamp collection: Sunday of the festival, 13:00–15:00. * Prize draw: Lacrosse Yoshiwara 2F, Sunday, 13:00–15:00.
== Additional Notes == * Due to the [[:en:Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]], the festival in 2011 was limited to mikoshi rituals, with taiko performances and food stalls canceled. * In 2023, to prevent the involvement of {{ill|yakuza|ja|暴力団|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yakuza|1=Japanese organized crime group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11090465}}, stall owners and workers were required to display photo IDs.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/20230611-OYT1T50131/2/ |title=Preventing Organized Crime at Festivals: Yomiuri Shimbun |publisher=Yomiuri Shimbun |date=June 11, 2023 |accessdate=June 12, 2023}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == See Also == * [[:en:Yoshiwara-juku|Yoshiwara-juku]] * {{ill|Yoshiwara Shopping Street|ja|吉原商店街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Shopping Street|1=Yoshiwara Shopping Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133934514}} * [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]]
== External Links == * [http://www.yoshiwara.net/gion/ Yoshiwara Gion Festival] * [http://yoshiwara-shoutengai.com/ Yoshiwara Shopping Street Promotion Association] * [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=X8yxreVhew8&feature=youtu.be Yoshiwara Gion Festival Introduction Video by Fuji City] {{Event-stub}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yoshiwara Gion-sai]][[ja:吉原祇園祭]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11412729]] Wikidata name: Yoshiwara Gion-sai [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox_holiday | Event Name = Yoshiwara Gion Festival | English Title = Yoshiwara Gion-sai | image = | image Size = | image Description = | Type = {{ill|Festival|ja|祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭|1=festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132241}} | Nickname = Oten no San | Official Name = | Former Name = | Date = 2nd Saturday and Sunday of June | Last Held = 2022 | Time = 15:00 - 21:00 | Venue = Around the Yoshiwara Shopping Street | Organized By = Yoshiwara Gion Festival Executive Committee | Co-organizers = | Sponsors = | Support = | Cooperation = | Management = | Access Name = | Nearest Station = {{ill|Gakunan Railway|ja|岳南鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakunan Railway|1=Gakunan Railway Company Ltd.|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24886051}} [[:en:Yoshiwara-honchō Station|Yoshiwara Honmachi Station]] | Direct Bus = | Parking = | URL = http://www.yoshiwara.net/gion/ | Notes = }}
'''Yoshiwara Gion Festival''' (吉原祇園祭, Yoshiwara Gion-sai) is a festival held annually on the second Saturday and Sunday of June in the Yoshiwara district of [[:en:Fuji, Shizuoka|Fuji, Shizuoka]], formerly [[:en:Yoshiwara, Shizuoka|Yoshiwara City]], Shizuoka Prefecture.
== Overview == * One of the historically long-standing festivals originating during the time of Yoshiwara City. * Known locally as "Oten no San," it is called the foremost Gion Festival in the Tōkai region. * The primary venue, {{ill|Yoshiwara Shopping Street|ja|吉原商店街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Shopping Street|1=Yoshiwara Shopping Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133934514}}, stretches approximately 1 km with over 200 stalls lining the streets, attracting more than 200,000 visitors over two days. * Although named a [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]], it is not a direct continuation of Kyoto's Gion Festival. The style of the festival reflects influences from various local traditions, as Yoshiwara was a {{ill|Shukuba|lt=Shukuba|lt=Post town|ja|宿場町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/post town|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宿場}}.
== Venue == * Centered around {{ill|Yoshiwara Shopping Street|ja|吉原商店街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Shopping Street|1=Yoshiwara Shopping Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133934514}} in the former Yoshiwara city center (now the central area of Fuji City). * From 13:00 to 21:00 during the festival, the shopping street is closed to vehicular traffic. * Access: ** Train: Alight at [[:en:Yoshiwara-honchō Station|Yoshiwara Honmachi Station]] on the {{ill|Gakunan Railway Line|ja|岳南電車岳南線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakunan Railway Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岳南電車岳南鉄道線|1=Gakunan Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5517715}}. ** Bus: Alight at {{ill|Yoshiwara Chūō Station|ja|吉原中央駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Chūō Station|1=Yoshiwara Chūō Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412690}} on {{ill|Fujikyu Shizuoka Bus|ja|富士急静岡バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujikyu Shizuoka Bus|1=Fujikyu Shizuoka Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11456063}}.
== Floats == [[File:Dashi.jpg|thumb|300px|Parading floats]] * Reflecting the cultural crossroads of the Tōkaidō, the festival features three types of floats: "traditional float," "stage float," and a hybrid combining elements of both. These 21 unique floats parade through the town. * The tradition reportedly began in the third year of Manji (1660) when a shopkeeper, having prayed to the deity [[:en:Tenjin|Tenjin]], fulfilled a vow by donating a shrine. During the year's Tenjin festival, residents mounted wheels on boats and pulled them in a procession.
== Mikoshi (Portable Shrine) == * The [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]], representing {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオノミコト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, is known for its fierce and dynamic style, earning the nickname "Kenka Mikoshi" (Fighting Shrine). * In the past, the mikoshi was handled so violently that it occasionally damaged houses. Locals refer to carrying the mikoshi as "shaking" rather than carrying, reflecting its vigorous movement. * Bamboo leaves attached to the mikoshi are considered talismans for warding off evil when displayed at home.<ref group="Note">This belief originates from the {{ill|Somin Shōrai legend|ja|蘇民将来伝説|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Somin Shōrai legend|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which says Susanoo will protect homes marked with bamboo leaves.</ref>
== Taiko Drumming == [[File:Miyadaiko.jpg|thumb|300px|Shrine Drums]] * Performed on {{ill|Nagadō Taiko|ja|長胴太鼓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagadō Taiko|1=nagadō daiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653585}} by two drummers, originally used to alert the community to the mikoshi's arrival. Known as "Call Drums" or "Fure Drums." * On Saturday evening, representatives from various neighborhoods compete in performances across Yoshiwara Shopping Street. * On Sunday, the drums fulfill their traditional role as "Call Drums" preceding the mikoshi.
== Oten no San Stamp Rally == * Visitors collect stamps at neighborhood venues marked in the festival guide. Collecting at least 10 stamps qualifies for a prize draw at Lacrosse Yoshiwara 2F Community F. * Stamp collection: Sunday of the festival, 13:00–15:00. * Prize draw: Lacrosse Yoshiwara 2F, Sunday, 13:00–15:00.
== Additional Notes == * Due to the [[:en:Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]], the festival in 2011 was limited to mikoshi rituals, with taiko performances and food stalls canceled. * In 2023, to prevent the involvement of {{ill|yakuza|ja|暴力団|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yakuza|1=Japanese organized crime group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11090465}}, stall owners and workers were required to display photo IDs.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/20230611-OYT1T50131/2/ |title=Preventing Organized Crime at Festivals: Yomiuri Shimbun |publisher=Yomiuri Shimbun |date=June 11, 2023 |accessdate=June 12, 2023}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == See Also == * [[:en:Yoshiwara-juku|Yoshiwara-juku]] * {{ill|Yoshiwara Shopping Street|ja|吉原商店街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Shopping Street|1=Yoshiwara Shopping Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133934514}} * [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]]
== External Links == * [http://www.yoshiwara.net/gion/ Yoshiwara Gion Festival] * [http://yoshiwara-shoutengai.com/ Yoshiwara Shopping Street Promotion Association] * [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=X8yxreVhew8&feature=youtu.be Yoshiwara Gion Festival Introduction Video by Fuji City] {{Event-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|吉原祇園祭|version=102383627|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Yoshiwara Gion-sai]][[ja:吉原祇園祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yoshiwara Gion-sai]][[ja:吉原祇園祭]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11412729]] Wikidata name: Yoshiwara Gion-sai [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox_holiday | Event Name = Yoshiwara Gion Festival | English Title = Yoshiwara Gion-sai | image = | image Size = | image Description = | Type = {{ill|Festival|ja|祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭|1=festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132241}} | Nickname = Oten no San | Official Name = | Former Name = | Date = 2nd Saturday and Sunday of June | Last Held = 2022 | Time = 15:00 - 21:00 | Venue = Around the Yoshiwara Shopping Street | Organized By = Yoshiwara Gion Festival Executive Committee | Co-organizers = | Sponsors = | Support = | Cooperation = | Management = | Access Name = | Nearest Station = {{ill|Gakunan Railway|ja|岳南鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakunan Railway|1=Gakunan Railway Company Ltd.|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24886051}} [[:en:Yoshiwara-honchō Station|Yoshiwara Honmachi Station]] | Direct Bus = | Parking = | URL = http://www.yoshiwara.net/gion/ | Notes = }}
'''Yoshiwara Gion Festival''' (吉原祇園祭, Yoshiwara Gion-sai) is a festival held annually on the second Saturday and Sunday of June in the Yoshiwara district of [[:en:Fuji, Shizuoka|Fuji, Shizuoka]], formerly [[:en:Yoshiwara, Shizuoka|Yoshiwara City]], Shizuoka Prefecture.
== Overview == * One of the historically long-standing festivals originating during the time of Yoshiwara City. * Known locally as "Oten no San," it is called the foremost Gion Festival in the Tōkai region. * The primary venue, {{ill|Yoshiwara Shopping Street|ja|吉原商店街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Shopping Street|1=Yoshiwara Shopping Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133934514}}, stretches approximately 1 km with over 200 stalls lining the streets, attracting more than 200,000 visitors over two days. * Although named a [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]], it is not a direct continuation of Kyoto's Gion Festival. The style of the festival reflects influences from various local traditions, as Yoshiwara was a {{ill|Shukuba|lt=Shukuba|lt=Post town|ja|宿場町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/post town|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宿場}}.
== Venue == * Centered around {{ill|Yoshiwara Shopping Street|ja|吉原商店街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Shopping Street|1=Yoshiwara Shopping Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133934514}} in the former Yoshiwara city center (now the central area of Fuji City). * From 13:00 to 21:00 during the festival, the shopping street is closed to vehicular traffic. * Access: ** Train: Alight at [[:en:Yoshiwara-honchō Station|Yoshiwara Honmachi Station]] on the {{ill|Gakunan Railway Line|ja|岳南電車岳南線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakunan Railway Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岳南電車岳南鉄道線|1=Gakunan Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5517715}}. ** Bus: Alight at {{ill|Yoshiwara Chūō Station|ja|吉原中央駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Chūō Station|1=Yoshiwara Chūō Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412690}} on {{ill|Fujikyu Shizuoka Bus|ja|富士急静岡バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujikyu Shizuoka Bus|1=Fujikyu Shizuoka Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11456063}}.
== Floats == [[File:Dashi.jpg|thumb|300px|Parading floats]] * Reflecting the cultural crossroads of the Tōkaidō, the festival features three types of floats: "traditional float," "stage float," and a hybrid combining elements of both. These 21 unique floats parade through the town. * The tradition reportedly began in the third year of Manji (1660) when a shopkeeper, having prayed to the deity [[:en:Tenjin|Tenjin]], fulfilled a vow by donating a shrine. During the year's Tenjin festival, residents mounted wheels on boats and pulled them in a procession.
== Mikoshi (Portable Shrine) == * The [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]], representing {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオノミコト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, is known for its fierce and dynamic style, earning the nickname "Kenka Mikoshi" (Fighting Shrine). * In the past, the mikoshi was handled so violently that it occasionally damaged houses. Locals refer to carrying the mikoshi as "shaking" rather than carrying, reflecting its vigorous movement. * Bamboo leaves attached to the mikoshi are considered talismans for warding off evil when displayed at home.<ref group="Note">This belief originates from the {{ill|Somin Shōrai legend|ja|蘇民将来伝説|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Somin Shōrai legend|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which says Susanoo will protect homes marked with bamboo leaves.</ref>
== Taiko Drumming == [[File:Miyadaiko.jpg|thumb|300px|Shrine Drums]] * Performed on {{ill|Nagadō Taiko|ja|長胴太鼓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagadō Taiko|1=nagadō daiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653585}} by two drummers, originally used to alert the community to the mikoshi's arrival. Known as "Call Drums" or "Fure Drums." * On Saturday evening, representatives from various neighborhoods compete in performances across Yoshiwara Shopping Street. * On Sunday, the drums fulfill their traditional role as "Call Drums" preceding the mikoshi.
== Oten no San Stamp Rally == * Visitors collect stamps at neighborhood venues marked in the festival guide. Collecting at least 10 stamps qualifies for a prize draw at Lacrosse Yoshiwara 2F Community F. * Stamp collection: Sunday of the festival, 13:00–15:00. * Prize draw: Lacrosse Yoshiwara 2F, Sunday, 13:00–15:00.
== Additional Notes == * Due to the [[:en:Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]], the festival in 2011 was limited to mikoshi rituals, with taiko performances and food stalls canceled. * In 2023, to prevent the involvement of {{ill|yakuza|ja|暴力団|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yakuza|1=Japanese organized crime group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11090465}}, stall owners and workers were required to display photo IDs.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/20230611-OYT1T50131/2/ |title=Preventing Organized Crime at Festivals: Yomiuri Shimbun |publisher=Yomiuri Shimbun |date=June 11, 2023 |accessdate=June 12, 2023}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == See Also == * [[:en:Yoshiwara-juku|Yoshiwara-juku]] * {{ill|Yoshiwara Shopping Street|ja|吉原商店街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Shopping Street|1=Yoshiwara Shopping Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133934514}} * [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]]
== External Links == * [http://www.yoshiwara.net/gion/ Yoshiwara Gion Festival] * [http://yoshiwara-shoutengai.com/ Yoshiwara Shopping Street Promotion Association] * [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=X8yxreVhew8&feature=youtu.be Yoshiwara Gion Festival Introduction Video by Fuji City] {{Event-stub}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Yoshiwara Gion-sai]][[ja:吉原祇園祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Yoshiwara Gion-sai]][[ja:吉原祇園祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yoshiwara Gion-sai]][[ja:吉原祇園祭]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11412729]] Wikidata name: Yoshiwara Gion-sai [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox_holiday | Event Name = Yoshiwara Gion Festival | English Title = Yoshiwara Gion-sai | image = | image Size = | image Description = | Type = {{ill|Festival|ja|祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭|1=festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132241}} | Nickname = Oten no San | Official Name = | Former Name = | Date = 2nd Saturday and Sunday of June | Last Held = 2022 | Time = 15:00 - 21:00 | Venue = Around the Yoshiwara Shopping Street | Organized By = Yoshiwara Gion Festival Executive Committee | Co-organizers = | Sponsors = | Support = | Cooperation = | Management = | Access Name = | Nearest Station = {{ill|Gakunan Railway|ja|岳南鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakunan Railway|1=Gakunan Railway Company Ltd.|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24886051}} [[:en:Yoshiwara-honchō Station|Yoshiwara Honmachi Station]] | Direct Bus = | Parking = | URL = http://www.yoshiwara.net/gion/ | Notes = }}
'''Yoshiwara Gion Festival''' (吉原祇園祭, Yoshiwara Gion-sai) is a festival held annually on the second Saturday and Sunday of June in the Yoshiwara district of [[:en:Fuji, Shizuoka|Fuji, Shizuoka]], formerly [[:en:Yoshiwara, Shizuoka|Yoshiwara City]], Shizuoka Prefecture.
== Overview == * One of the historically long-standing festivals originating during the time of Yoshiwara City. * Known locally as "Oten no San," it is called the foremost Gion Festival in the Tōkai region. * The primary venue, {{ill|Yoshiwara Shopping Street|ja|吉原商店街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Shopping Street|1=Yoshiwara Shopping Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133934514}}, stretches approximately 1 km with over 200 stalls lining the streets, attracting more than 200,000 visitors over two days. * Although named a [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]], it is not a direct continuation of Kyoto's Gion Festival. The style of the festival reflects influences from various local traditions, as Yoshiwara was a {{ill|Shukuba|lt=Shukuba|lt=Post town|ja|宿場町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/post town|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宿場}}.
== Venue == * Centered around {{ill|Yoshiwara Shopping Street|ja|吉原商店街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Shopping Street|1=Yoshiwara Shopping Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133934514}} in the former Yoshiwara city center (now the central area of Fuji City). * From 13:00 to 21:00 during the festival, the shopping street is closed to vehicular traffic. * Access: ** Train: Alight at [[:en:Yoshiwara-honchō Station|Yoshiwara Honmachi Station]] on the {{ill|Gakunan Railway Line|ja|岳南電車岳南線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakunan Railway Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岳南電車岳南鉄道線|1=Gakunan Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5517715}}. ** Bus: Alight at {{ill|Yoshiwara Chūō Station|ja|吉原中央駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Chūō Station|1=Yoshiwara Chūō Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412690}} on {{ill|Fujikyu Shizuoka Bus|ja|富士急静岡バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujikyu Shizuoka Bus|1=Fujikyu Shizuoka Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11456063}}.
== Floats == [[File:Dashi.jpg|thumb|300px|Parading floats]] * Reflecting the cultural crossroads of the Tōkaidō, the festival features three types of floats: "traditional float," "stage float," and a hybrid combining elements of both. These 21 unique floats parade through the town. * The tradition reportedly began in the third year of Manji (1660) when a shopkeeper, having prayed to the deity [[:en:Tenjin|Tenjin]], fulfilled a vow by donating a shrine. During the year's Tenjin festival, residents mounted wheels on boats and pulled them in a procession.
== Mikoshi (Portable Shrine) == * The [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]], representing {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオノミコト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, is known for its fierce and dynamic style, earning the nickname "Kenka Mikoshi" (Fighting Shrine). * In the past, the mikoshi was handled so violently that it occasionally damaged houses. Locals refer to carrying the mikoshi as "shaking" rather than carrying, reflecting its vigorous movement. * Bamboo leaves attached to the mikoshi are considered talismans for warding off evil when displayed at home.<ref group="Note">This belief originates from the {{ill|Somin Shōrai legend|ja|蘇民将来伝説|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Somin Shōrai legend|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which says Susanoo will protect homes marked with bamboo leaves.</ref>
== Taiko Drumming == [[File:Miyadaiko.jpg|thumb|300px|Shrine Drums]] * Performed on {{ill|Nagadō Taiko|ja|長胴太鼓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagadō Taiko|1=nagadō daiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653585}} by two drummers, originally used to alert the community to the mikoshi's arrival. Known as "Call Drums" or "Fure Drums." * On Saturday evening, representatives from various neighborhoods compete in performances across Yoshiwara Shopping Street. * On Sunday, the drums fulfill their traditional role as "Call Drums" preceding the mikoshi.
== Oten no San Stamp Rally == * Visitors collect stamps at neighborhood venues marked in the festival guide. Collecting at least 10 stamps qualifies for a prize draw at Lacrosse Yoshiwara 2F Community F. * Stamp collection: Sunday of the festival, 13:00–15:00. * Prize draw: Lacrosse Yoshiwara 2F, Sunday, 13:00–15:00.
== Additional Notes == * Due to the [[:en:Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]], the festival in 2011 was limited to mikoshi rituals, with taiko performances and food stalls canceled. * In 2023, to prevent the involvement of {{ill|yakuza|ja|暴力団|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yakuza|1=Japanese organized crime group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11090465}}, stall owners and workers were required to display photo IDs.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/20230611-OYT1T50131/2/ |title=Preventing Organized Crime at Festivals: Yomiuri Shimbun |publisher=Yomiuri Shimbun |date=June 11, 2023 |accessdate=June 12, 2023}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == See Also == * [[:en:Yoshiwara-juku|Yoshiwara-juku]] * {{ill|Yoshiwara Shopping Street|ja|吉原商店街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Shopping Street|1=Yoshiwara Shopping Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133934514}} * [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]]
== External Links == * [http://www.yoshiwara.net/gion/ Yoshiwara Gion Festival] * [http://yoshiwara-shoutengai.com/ Yoshiwara Shopping Street Promotion Association] * [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=X8yxreVhew8&feature=youtu.be Yoshiwara Gion Festival Introduction Video by Fuji City] {{Event-stub}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Yoshiwara Gion-sai]][[ja:吉原祇園祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Yoshiwara Gion-sai]][[ja:吉原祇園祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yoshiwara Gion-sai]][[ja:吉原祇園祭]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11412729]] Wikidata name: Yoshiwara Gion-sai [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox_holiday | Event Name = Yoshiwara Gion Festival | English Title = Yoshiwara Gion-sai | image = | image Size = | image Description = | Type = {{ill|Festival|ja|祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭|1=festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132241}} | Nickname = Oten no San | Official Name = | Former Name = | Date = 2nd Saturday and Sunday of June | Last Held = 2022 | Time = 15:00 - 21:00 | Venue = Around the Yoshiwara Shopping Street | Organized By = Yoshiwara Gion Festival Executive Committee | Co-organizers = | Sponsors = | Support = | Cooperation = | Management = | Access Name = | Nearest Station = {{ill|Gakunan Railway|ja|岳南鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakunan Railway|1=Gakunan Railway Company Ltd.|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24886051}} [[:en:Yoshiwara-honchō Station|Yoshiwara Honmachi Station]] | Direct Bus = | Parking = | URL = http://www.yoshiwara.net/gion/ | Notes = }}
'''Yoshiwara Gion Festival''' (吉原祇園祭, Yoshiwara Gion-sai) is a festival held annually on the second Saturday and Sunday of June in the Yoshiwara district of [[:en:Fuji, Shizuoka|Fuji, Shizuoka]], formerly [[:en:Yoshiwara, Shizuoka|Yoshiwara City]], Shizuoka Prefecture.
== Overview == * One of the historically long-standing festivals originating during the time of Yoshiwara City. * Known locally as "Oten no San," it is called the foremost Gion Festival in the Tōkai region. * The primary venue, {{ill|Yoshiwara Shopping Street|ja|吉原商店街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Shopping Street|1=Yoshiwara Shopping Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133934514}}, stretches approximately 1 km with over 200 stalls lining the streets, attracting more than 200,000 visitors over two days. * Although named a [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]], it is not a direct continuation of Kyoto's Gion Festival. The style of the festival reflects influences from various local traditions, as Yoshiwara was a {{ill|Shukuba|lt=Shukuba|lt=Post town|ja|宿場町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/post town|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宿場}}.
== Venue == * Centered around {{ill|Yoshiwara Shopping Street|ja|吉原商店街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Shopping Street|1=Yoshiwara Shopping Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133934514}} in the former Yoshiwara city center (now the central area of Fuji City). * From 13:00 to 21:00 during the festival, the shopping street is closed to vehicular traffic. * Access: ** Train: Alight at [[:en:Yoshiwara-honchō Station|Yoshiwara Honmachi Station]] on the {{ill|Gakunan Railway Line|ja|岳南電車岳南線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakunan Railway Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岳南電車岳南鉄道線|1=Gakunan Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5517715}}. ** Bus: Alight at {{ill|Yoshiwara Chūō Station|ja|吉原中央駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Chūō Station|1=Yoshiwara Chūō Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412690}} on {{ill|Fujikyu Shizuoka Bus|ja|富士急静岡バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujikyu Shizuoka Bus|1=Fujikyu Shizuoka Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11456063}}.
== Floats == [[File:Dashi.jpg|thumb|300px|Parading floats]] * Reflecting the cultural crossroads of the Tōkaidō, the festival features three types of floats: "traditional float," "stage float," and a hybrid combining elements of both. These 21 unique floats parade through the town. * The tradition reportedly began in the third year of Manji (1660) when a shopkeeper, having prayed to the deity [[:en:Tenjin|Tenjin]], fulfilled a vow by donating a shrine. During the year's Tenjin festival, residents mounted wheels on boats and pulled them in a procession.
== Mikoshi (Portable Shrine) == * The [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]], representing {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオノミコト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, is known for its fierce and dynamic style, earning the nickname "Kenka Mikoshi" (Fighting Shrine). * In the past, the mikoshi was handled so violently that it occasionally damaged houses. Locals refer to carrying the mikoshi as "shaking" rather than carrying, reflecting its vigorous movement. * Bamboo leaves attached to the mikoshi are considered talismans for warding off evil when displayed at home.<ref group="Note">This belief originates from the {{ill|Somin Shōrai legend|ja|蘇民将来伝説|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Somin Shōrai legend|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which says Susanoo will protect homes marked with bamboo leaves.</ref>
== Taiko Drumming == [[File:Miyadaiko.jpg|thumb|300px|Shrine Drums]] * Performed on {{ill|Nagadō Taiko|ja|長胴太鼓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagadō Taiko|1=nagadō daiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653585}} by two drummers, originally used to alert the community to the mikoshi's arrival. Known as "Call Drums" or "Fure Drums." * On Saturday evening, representatives from various neighborhoods compete in performances across Yoshiwara Shopping Street. * On Sunday, the drums fulfill their traditional role as "Call Drums" preceding the mikoshi.
== Oten no San Stamp Rally == * Visitors collect stamps at neighborhood venues marked in the festival guide. Collecting at least 10 stamps qualifies for a prize draw at Lacrosse Yoshiwara 2F Community F. * Stamp collection: Sunday of the festival, 13:00–15:00. * Prize draw: Lacrosse Yoshiwara 2F, Sunday, 13:00–15:00.
== Additional Notes == * Due to the [[:en:Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]], the festival in 2011 was limited to mikoshi rituals, with taiko performances and food stalls canceled. * In 2023, to prevent the involvement of {{ill|yakuza|ja|暴力団|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yakuza|1=Japanese organized crime group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11090465}}, stall owners and workers were required to display photo IDs.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/20230611-OYT1T50131/2/ |title=Preventing Organized Crime at Festivals: Yomiuri Shimbun |publisher=Yomiuri Shimbun |date=June 11, 2023 |accessdate=June 12, 2023}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == See Also == * [[:en:Yoshiwara-juku|Yoshiwara-juku]] * {{ill|Yoshiwara Shopping Street|ja|吉原商店街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Shopping Street|1=Yoshiwara Shopping Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133934514}} * [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]]
== External Links == * [http://www.yoshiwara.net/gion/ Yoshiwara Gion Festival] * [http://yoshiwara-shoutengai.com/ Yoshiwara Shopping Street Promotion Association] * [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=X8yxreVhew8&feature=youtu.be Yoshiwara Gion Festival Introduction Video by Fuji City] {{Event-stub}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Yoshiwara Gion-sai]][[ja:吉原祇園祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Yoshiwara Gion-sai]][[ja:吉原祇園祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yoshiwara Gion-sai]][[ja:吉原祇園祭]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11412729]] Wikidata name: Yoshiwara Gion-sai [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox_holiday | Event Name = Yoshiwara Gion Festival | English Title = Yoshiwara Gion-sai | image = | image Size = | image Description = | Type = {{ill|Festival|ja|祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭|1=festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132241}} | Nickname = Oten no San | Official Name = | Former Name = | Date = 2nd Saturday and Sunday of June | Last Held = 2022 | Time = 15:00 - 21:00 | Venue = Around the Yoshiwara Shopping Street | Organized By = Yoshiwara Gion Festival Executive Committee | Co-organizers = | Sponsors = | Support = | Cooperation = | Management = | Access Name = | Nearest Station = {{ill|Gakunan Railway|ja|岳南鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakunan Railway|1=Gakunan Railway Company Ltd.|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24886051}} [[:en:Yoshiwara-honchō Station|Yoshiwara Honmachi Station]] | Direct Bus = | Parking = | URL = http://www.yoshiwara.net/gion/ | Notes = }}
'''Yoshiwara Gion Festival''' (吉原祇園祭, Yoshiwara Gion-sai) is a festival held annually on the second Saturday and Sunday of June in the Yoshiwara district of [[:en:Fuji, Shizuoka|Fuji, Shizuoka]], formerly [[:en:Yoshiwara, Shizuoka|Yoshiwara City]], Shizuoka Prefecture.
== Overview == * One of the historically long-standing festivals originating during the time of Yoshiwara City. * Known locally as "Oten no San," it is called the foremost Gion Festival in the Tōkai region. * The primary venue, {{ill|Yoshiwara Shopping Street|ja|吉原商店街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Shopping Street|1=Yoshiwara Shopping Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133934514}}, stretches approximately 1 km with over 200 stalls lining the streets, attracting more than 200,000 visitors over two days. * Although named a [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]], it is not a direct continuation of Kyoto's Gion Festival. The style of the festival reflects influences from various local traditions, as Yoshiwara was a {{ill|Shukuba|lt=Shukuba|lt=Post town|ja|宿場町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/post town|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宿場}}.
== Venue == * Centered around {{ill|Yoshiwara Shopping Street|ja|吉原商店街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Shopping Street|1=Yoshiwara Shopping Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133934514}} in the former Yoshiwara city center (now the central area of Fuji City). * From 13:00 to 21:00 during the festival, the shopping street is closed to vehicular traffic. * Access: ** Train: Alight at [[:en:Yoshiwara-honchō Station|Yoshiwara Honmachi Station]] on the {{ill|Gakunan Railway Line|ja|岳南電車岳南線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakunan Railway Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岳南電車岳南鉄道線|1=Gakunan Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5517715}}. ** Bus: Alight at {{ill|Yoshiwara Chūō Station|ja|吉原中央駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Chūō Station|1=Yoshiwara Chūō Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412690}} on {{ill|Fujikyu Shizuoka Bus|ja|富士急静岡バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujikyu Shizuoka Bus|1=Fujikyu Shizuoka Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11456063}}.
== Floats == [[File:Dashi.jpg|thumb|300px|Parading floats]] * Reflecting the cultural crossroads of the Tōkaidō, the festival features three types of floats: "traditional float," "stage float," and a hybrid combining elements of both. These 21 unique floats parade through the town. * The tradition reportedly began in the third year of Manji (1660) when a shopkeeper, having prayed to the deity [[:en:Tenjin|Tenjin]], fulfilled a vow by donating a shrine. During the year's Tenjin festival, residents mounted wheels on boats and pulled them in a procession.
== Mikoshi (Portable Shrine) == * The [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]], representing {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオノミコト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, is known for its fierce and dynamic style, earning the nickname "Kenka Mikoshi" (Fighting Shrine). * In the past, the mikoshi was handled so violently that it occasionally damaged houses. Locals refer to carrying the mikoshi as "shaking" rather than carrying, reflecting its vigorous movement. * Bamboo leaves attached to the mikoshi are considered talismans for warding off evil when displayed at home.<ref group="Note">This belief originates from the {{ill|Somin Shōrai legend|ja|蘇民将来伝説|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Somin Shōrai legend|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which says Susanoo will protect homes marked with bamboo leaves.</ref>
== Taiko Drumming == [[File:Miyadaiko.jpg|thumb|300px|Shrine Drums]] * Performed on {{ill|Nagadō Taiko|ja|長胴太鼓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagadō Taiko|1=nagadō daiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653585}} by two drummers, originally used to alert the community to the mikoshi's arrival. Known as "Call Drums" or "Fure Drums." * On Saturday evening, representatives from various neighborhoods compete in performances across Yoshiwara Shopping Street. * On Sunday, the drums fulfill their traditional role as "Call Drums" preceding the mikoshi.
== Oten no San Stamp Rally == * Visitors collect stamps at neighborhood venues marked in the festival guide. Collecting at least 10 stamps qualifies for a prize draw at Lacrosse Yoshiwara 2F Community F. * Stamp collection: Sunday of the festival, 13:00–15:00. * Prize draw: Lacrosse Yoshiwara 2F, Sunday, 13:00–15:00.
== Additional Notes == * Due to the [[:en:Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]], the festival in 2011 was limited to mikoshi rituals, with taiko performances and food stalls canceled. * In 2023, to prevent the involvement of {{ill|yakuza|ja|暴力団|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yakuza|1=Japanese organized crime group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11090465}}, stall owners and workers were required to display photo IDs.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/20230611-OYT1T50131/2/ |title=Preventing Organized Crime at Festivals: Yomiuri Shimbun |publisher=Yomiuri Shimbun |date=June 11, 2023 |accessdate=June 12, 2023}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == See Also == * [[:en:Yoshiwara-juku|Yoshiwara-juku]] * {{ill|Yoshiwara Shopping Street|ja|吉原商店街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Shopping Street|1=Yoshiwara Shopping Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133934514}} * [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]]
== External Links == * [http://www.yoshiwara.net/gion/ Yoshiwara Gion Festival] * [http://yoshiwara-shoutengai.com/ Yoshiwara Shopping Street Promotion Association] * [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=X8yxreVhew8&feature=youtu.be Yoshiwara Gion Festival Introduction Video by Fuji City] {{Event-stub}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Yoshiwara Gion-sai]][[ja:吉原祇園祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Yoshiwara Gion-sai]][[ja:吉原祇園祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yoshiwara Gion-sai]][[ja:吉原祇園祭]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11412729]] Wikidata name: Yoshiwara Gion-sai [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox_holiday | Event Name = Yoshiwara Gion Festival | English Title = Yoshiwara Gion-sai | image = | image Size = | image Description = | Type = {{ill|Festival|ja|祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭|1=festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132241}} | Nickname = Oten no San | Official Name = | Former Name = | Date = 2nd Saturday and Sunday of June | Last Held = 2022 | Time = 15:00 - 21:00 | Venue = Around the Yoshiwara Shopping Street | Organized By = Yoshiwara Gion Festival Executive Committee | Co-organizers = | Sponsors = | Support = | Cooperation = | Management = | Access Name = | Nearest Station = {{ill|Gakunan Railway|ja|岳南鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakunan Railway|1=Gakunan Railway Company Ltd.|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24886051}} {{ill|Yoshiwara-honchō Station|en|Yoshiwara-honchō Station|WD=Q8056327|lt=Yoshiwara Honmachi Station}} | Direct Bus = | Parking = | URL = http://www.yoshiwara.net/gion/ | Notes = }}
'''Yoshiwara Gion Festival''' (吉原祇園祭, Yoshiwara Gion-sai) is a festival held annually on the second Saturday and Sunday of June in the Yoshiwara district of {{ill|Fuji, Shizuoka|en|Fuji, Shizuoka|WD=Q328613|lt=Fuji, Shizuoka}}, formerly {{ill|Yoshiwara, Shizuoka|en|Yoshiwara, Shizuoka|WD=Q997390|lt=Yoshiwara City}}, Shizuoka Prefecture.
== Overview == * One of the historically long-standing festivals originating during the time of Yoshiwara City. * Known locally as "Oten no San," it is called the foremost Gion Festival in the Tōkai region. * The primary venue, {{ill|Yoshiwara Shopping Street|ja|吉原商店街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Shopping Street|1=Yoshiwara Shopping Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133934514}}, stretches approximately 1 km with over 200 stalls lining the streets, attracting more than 200,000 visitors over two days. * Although named a {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en|Gion Matsuri|WD=Q979873|lt=Gion Festival}}, it is not a direct continuation of Kyoto's Gion Festival. The style of the festival reflects influences from various local traditions, as Yoshiwara was a {{ill|Shukuba|lt=Shukuba|lt=Post town|ja|宿場町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/post town|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宿場}}.
== Venue == * Centered around {{ill|Yoshiwara Shopping Street|ja|吉原商店街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Shopping Street|1=Yoshiwara Shopping Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133934514}} in the former Yoshiwara city center (now the central area of Fuji City). * From 13:00 to 21:00 during the festival, the shopping street is closed to vehicular traffic. * Access: ** Train: Alight at {{ill|Yoshiwara-honchō Station|en|Yoshiwara-honchō Station|WD=Q8056327|lt=Yoshiwara Honmachi Station}} on the {{ill|Gakunan Railway Line|ja|岳南電車岳南線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakunan Railway Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岳南電車岳南鉄道線|1=Gakunan Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5517715}}. ** Bus: Alight at {{ill|Yoshiwara Chūō Station|ja|吉原中央駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Chūō Station|1=Yoshiwara Chūō Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412690}} on {{ill|Fujikyu Shizuoka Bus|ja|富士急静岡バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujikyu Shizuoka Bus|1=Fujikyu Shizuoka Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11456063}}.
== Floats == [[File:Dashi.jpg|thumb|300px|Parading floats]] * Reflecting the cultural crossroads of the Tōkaidō, the festival features three types of floats: "traditional float," "stage float," and a hybrid combining elements of both. These 21 unique floats parade through the town. * The tradition reportedly began in the third year of Manji (1660) when a shopkeeper, having prayed to the deity {{ill|Tenjin|en|Tenjin|WD=Q1305727|lt=Tenjin}}, fulfilled a vow by donating a shrine. During the year's Tenjin festival, residents mounted wheels on boats and pulled them in a procession.
== Mikoshi (Portable Shrine) == * The {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=Mikoshi}}, representing {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオノミコト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, is known for its fierce and dynamic style, earning the nickname "Kenka Mikoshi" (Fighting Shrine). * In the past, the mikoshi was handled so violently that it occasionally damaged houses. Locals refer to carrying the mikoshi as "shaking" rather than carrying, reflecting its vigorous movement. * Bamboo leaves attached to the mikoshi are considered talismans for warding off evil when displayed at home.<ref group="Note">This belief originates from the {{ill|Somin Shōrai legend|ja|蘇民将来伝説|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Somin Shōrai legend|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which says Susanoo will protect homes marked with bamboo leaves.</ref>
== Taiko Drumming == [[File:Miyadaiko.jpg|thumb|300px|Shrine Drums]] * Performed on {{ill|Nagadō Taiko|ja|長胴太鼓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagadō Taiko|1=nagadō daiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653585}} by two drummers, originally used to alert the community to the mikoshi's arrival. Known as "Call Drums" or "Fure Drums." * On Saturday evening, representatives from various neighborhoods compete in performances across Yoshiwara Shopping Street. * On Sunday, the drums fulfill their traditional role as "Call Drums" preceding the mikoshi.
== Oten no San Stamp Rally == * Visitors collect stamps at neighborhood venues marked in the festival guide. Collecting at least 10 stamps qualifies for a prize draw at Lacrosse Yoshiwara 2F Community F. * Stamp collection: Sunday of the festival, 13:00–15:00. * Prize draw: Lacrosse Yoshiwara 2F, Sunday, 13:00–15:00.
== Additional Notes == * Due to the {{ill|Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|en|Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|WD=Q1136168|lt=2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami}}, the festival in 2011 was limited to mikoshi rituals, with taiko performances and food stalls canceled. * In 2023, to prevent the involvement of {{ill|yakuza|ja|暴力団|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yakuza|1=Japanese organized crime group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11090465}}, stall owners and workers were required to display photo IDs.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/20230611-OYT1T50131/2/ |title=Preventing Organized Crime at Festivals: Yomiuri Shimbun |publisher=Yomiuri Shimbun |date=June 11, 2023 |accessdate=June 12, 2023}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == See Also == * {{ill|Yoshiwara-juku|en|Yoshiwara-juku|WD=Q3069833|lt=Yoshiwara-juku}} * {{ill|Yoshiwara Shopping Street|ja|吉原商店街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Shopping Street|1=Yoshiwara Shopping Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133934514}} * {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en|Gion Matsuri|WD=Q979873|lt=Gion Festival}}
== External Links == * [http://www.yoshiwara.net/gion/ Yoshiwara Gion Festival] * [http://yoshiwara-shoutengai.com/ Yoshiwara Shopping Street Promotion Association] * [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=X8yxreVhew8&feature=youtu.be Yoshiwara Gion Festival Introduction Video by Fuji City] {{Event-stub}}

Collecting at least 10 stamps qualifies for a prize draw at Lacrosse Yoshiwara 2F Community F. * Stamp collection: Sunday of the festival, 13:00–15:00. * Prize draw: Lacrosse Yoshiwara 2F, Sunday, 13:00–15:00.
== Additional Notes == * Due to the {{ill|Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|en|Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|WD=Q1136168|lt=2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami}}, the festival in 2011 was limited to mikoshi rituals, with taiko performances and food stalls canceled. * In 2023, to prevent the involvement of {{ill|yakuza|ja|暴力団|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yakuza|1=Japanese organized crime group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11090465}}, stall owners and workers were required to display photo IDs.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/20230611-OYT1T50131/2/ |title=Preventing Organized Crime at Festivals: Yomiuri Shimbun |publisher=Yomiuri Shimbun |date=June 11, 2023 |accessdate=June 12, 2023}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == See Also == * {{ill|Yoshiwara-juku|en|Yoshiwara-juku|WD=Q3069833|lt=Yoshiwara-juku}} * {{ill|Yoshiwara Shopping Street|ja|吉原商店街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Shopping Street|1=Yoshiwara Shopping Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133934514}} * {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en|Gion Matsuri|WD=Q979873|lt=Gion Festival}}
== External Links == * [http://www.yoshiwara.net/gion/ Yoshiwara Gion Festival] * [http://yoshiwara-shoutengai.com/ Yoshiwara Shopping Street Promotion Association] * [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=X8yxreVhew8&feature=youtu.be Yoshiwara Gion Festival Introduction Video by Fuji City] {{Event-stub}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Yoshiwara Gion-sai]][[ja:吉原祇園祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Yoshiwara Gion-sai]][[ja:吉原祇園祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yoshiwara Gion-sai]][[ja:吉原祇園祭]]
{{Infobox_holiday | Event Name = Yoshiwara Gion Festival | English Title = Yoshiwara Gion-sai | image = | image Size = | image Description = | Type = {{ill|Festival|ja|祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭|1=festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132241}} | Nickname = Oten no San | Official Name = | Former Name = | Date = 2nd Saturday and Sunday of June | Last Held = 2022 | Time = 15:00 - 21:00 | Venue = Around the Yoshiwara Shopping Street | Organized By = Yoshiwara Gion Festival Executive Committee | Co-organizers = | Sponsors = | Support = | Cooperation = | Management = | Access Name = | Nearest Station = {{ill|Gakunan Railway|ja|岳南鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakunan Railway|1=Gakunan Railway Company Ltd.|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24886051}} {{ill|Yoshiwara-honchō Station|en|Yoshiwara-honchō Station|WD=Q8056327|lt=Yoshiwara Honmachi Station}} | Direct Bus = | Parking = | URL = http://www.yoshiwara.net/gion/ | Notes = }}
'''Yoshiwara Gion Festival''' (吉原祇園祭, Yoshiwara Gion-sai) is a festival held annually on the second Saturday and Sunday of June in the Yoshiwara district of {{ill|Fuji, Shizuoka|en|Fuji, Shizuoka|WD=Q328613|lt=Fuji, Shizuoka}}, formerly {{ill|Yoshiwara, Shizuoka|en|Yoshiwara, Shizuoka|WD=Q997390|lt=Yoshiwara City}}, Shizuoka Prefecture.
== Overview == * One of the historically long-standing festivals originating during the time of Yoshiwara City. * Known locally as "Oten no San," it is called the foremost Gion Festival in the Tōkai region. * The primary venue, {{ill|Yoshiwara Shopping Street|ja|吉原商店街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Shopping Street|1=Yoshiwara Shopping Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133934514}}, stretches approximately 1 km with over 200 stalls lining the streets, attracting more than 200,000 visitors over two days. * Although named a {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en|Gion Matsuri|WD=Q979873|lt=Gion Festival}}, it is not a direct continuation of Kyoto's Gion Festival. The style of the festival reflects influences from various local traditions, as Yoshiwara was a {{ill|Shukuba|lt=Shukuba|lt=Post town|ja|宿場町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/post town|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宿場}}.
== Venue == * Centered around {{ill|Yoshiwara Shopping Street|ja|吉原商店街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Shopping Street|1=Yoshiwara Shopping Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133934514}} in the former Yoshiwara city center (now the central area of Fuji City). * From 13:00 to 21:00 during the festival, the shopping street is closed to vehicular traffic. * Access: ** Train: Alight at {{ill|Yoshiwara-honchō Station|en|Yoshiwara-honchō Station|WD=Q8056327|lt=Yoshiwara Honmachi Station}} on the {{ill|Gakunan Railway Line|ja|岳南電車岳南線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakunan Railway Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岳南電車岳南鉄道線|1=Gakunan Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5517715}}. ** Bus: Alight at {{ill|Yoshiwara Chūō Station|ja|吉原中央駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Chūō Station|1=Yoshiwara Chūō Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412690}} on {{ill|Fujikyu Shizuoka Bus|ja|富士急静岡バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujikyu Shizuoka Bus|1=Fujikyu Shizuoka Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11456063}}.
== Floats == [[File:Dashi.jpg|thumb|300px|Parading floats]] * Reflecting the cultural crossroads of the Tōkaidō, the festival features three types of floats: "traditional float," "stage float," and a hybrid combining elements of both. These 21 unique floats parade through the town. * The tradition reportedly began in the third year of Manji (1660) when a shopkeeper, having prayed to the deity {{ill|Tenjin|en|Tenjin|WD=Q1305727|lt=Tenjin}}, fulfilled a vow by donating a shrine. During the year's Tenjin festival, residents mounted wheels on boats and pulled them in a procession.
== Mikoshi (Portable Shrine) == * The {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=Mikoshi}}, representing {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオノミコト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, is known for its fierce and dynamic style, earning the nickname "Kenka Mikoshi" (Fighting Shrine). * In the past, the mikoshi was handled so violently that it occasionally damaged houses. Locals refer to carrying the mikoshi as "shaking" rather than carrying, reflecting its vigorous movement. * Bamboo leaves attached to the mikoshi are considered talismans for warding off evil when displayed at home.<ref group="Note">This belief originates from the {{ill|Somin Shōrai legend|ja|蘇民将来伝説|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Somin Shōrai legend|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which says Susanoo will protect homes marked with bamboo leaves.</ref>
== Taiko Drumming == [[File:Miyadaiko.jpg|thumb|300px|Shrine Drums]] * Performed on {{ill|Nagadō Taiko|ja|長胴太鼓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagadō Taiko|1=nagadō daiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653585}} by two drummers, originally used to alert the community to the mikoshi's arrival. Known as "Call Drums" or "Fure Drums." * On Saturday evening, representatives from various neighborhoods compete in performances across Yoshiwara Shopping Street. * On Sunday, the drums fulfill their traditional role as "Call Drums" preceding the mikoshi.
== Oten no San Stamp Rally == * Visitors collect stamps at neighborhood venues marked in the festival guide. Collecting at least 10 stamps qualifies for a prize draw at Lacrosse Yoshiwara 2F Community F. * Stamp collection: Sunday of the festival, 13:00–15:00. * Prize draw: Lacrosse Yoshiwara 2F, Sunday, 13:00–15:00.
== Additional Notes == * Due to the {{ill|Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|en|Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|WD=Q1136168|lt=2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami}}, the festival in 2011 was limited to mikoshi rituals, with taiko performances and food stalls canceled. * In 2023, to prevent the involvement of {{ill|yakuza|ja|暴力団|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yakuza|1=Japanese organized crime group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11090465}}, stall owners and workers were required to display photo IDs.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/20230611-OYT1T50131/2/ |title=Preventing Organized Crime at Festivals: Yomiuri Shimbun |publisher=Yomiuri Shimbun |date=June 11, 2023 |accessdate=June 12, 2023}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == See Also == * {{ill|Yoshiwara-juku|en|Yoshiwara-juku|WD=Q3069833|lt=Yoshiwara-juku}} * {{ill|Yoshiwara Shopping Street|ja|吉原商店街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Shopping Street|1=Yoshiwara Shopping Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133934514}} * {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en|Gion Matsuri|WD=Q979873|lt=Gion Festival}}
== External Links == * [http://www.yoshiwara.net/gion/ Yoshiwara Gion Festival] * [http://yoshiwara-shoutengai.com/ Yoshiwara Shopping Street Promotion Association] * [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=X8yxreVhew8&feature=youtu.be Yoshiwara Gion Festival Introduction Video by Fuji City] {{Event-stub}}

== Mikoshi (Portable Shrine) == * The {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=Mikoshi|1=Mikoshi}}, representing {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオノミコト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, is known for its fierce and dynamic style, earning the nickname "Kenka Mikoshi" (Fighting Shrine). * In the past, the mikoshi was handled so violently that it occasionally damaged houses. Locals refer to carrying the mikoshi as "shaking" rather than carrying, reflecting its vigorous movement. * Bamboo leaves attached to the mikoshi are considered talismans for warding off evil when displayed at home.<ref group="Note">This belief originates from the {{ill|Somin Shōrai legend|ja|蘇民将来伝説|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Somin Shōrai legend|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which says Susanoo will protect homes marked with bamboo leaves.</ref>
== Taiko Drumming == [[File:Miyadaiko.jpg|thumb|300px|Shrine Drums]] * Performed on {{ill|Nagadō Taiko|ja|長胴太鼓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagadō Taiko|1=nagadō daiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653585}} by two drummers, originally used to alert the community to the mikoshi's arrival. Known as "Call Drums" or "Fure Drums." * On Saturday evening, representatives from various neighborhoods compete in performances across Yoshiwara Shopping Street. * On Sunday, the drums fulfill their traditional role as "Call Drums" preceding the mikoshi.
== Oten no San Stamp Rally == * Visitors collect stamps at neighborhood venues marked in the festival guide. Collecting at least 10 stamps qualifies for a prize draw at Lacrosse Yoshiwara 2F Community F. * Stamp collection: Sunday of the festival, 13:00–15:00. * Prize draw: Lacrosse Yoshiwara 2F, Sunday, 13:00–15:00.
== Additional Notes == * Due to the {{ill|Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|en|Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|WD=Q1136168|lt=2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|1=Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami}}, the festival in 2011 was limited to mikoshi rituals, with taiko performances and food stalls canceled. * In 2023, to prevent the involvement of {{ill|yakuza|ja|暴力団|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yakuza|1=Japanese organized crime group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11090465}}, stall owners and workers were required to display photo IDs.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/20230611-OYT1T50131/2/ |title=Preventing Organized Crime at Festivals: Yomiuri Shimbun |publisher=Yomiuri Shimbun |date=June 11, 2023 |accessdate=June 12, 2023}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == See Also == * {{ill|Yoshiwara-juku|en|Yoshiwara-juku|WD=Q3069833|lt=Yoshiwara-juku|1=Yoshiwara-juku}} * {{ill|Yoshiwara Shopping Street|ja|吉原商店街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Shopping Street|1=Yoshiwara Shopping Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133934514}} * {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en|Gion Matsuri|WD=Q979873|lt=Gion Festival|1=Gion Matsuri}}
== External Links == * [http://www.yoshiwara.net/gion/ Yoshiwara Gion Festival] * [http://yoshiwara-shoutengai.com/ Yoshiwara Shopping Street Promotion Association] * [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=X8yxreVhew8&feature=youtu.be Yoshiwara Gion Festival Introduction Video by Fuji City] {{Event-stub}}

{{Infobox_holiday | Event Name = Yoshiwara Gion Festival | English Title = Yoshiwara Gion-sai | image = | image Size = | image Description = | Type = {{ill|Festival|ja|祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭|1=Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132241}} | Nickname = Oten no San | Official Name = | Former Name = | Date = 2nd Saturday and Sunday of June | Last Held = 2022 | Time = 15:00 - 21:00 | Venue = Around the Yoshiwara Shopping Street | Organized By = Yoshiwara Gion Festival Executive Committee | Co-organizers = | Sponsors = | Support = | Cooperation = | Management = | Access Name = | Nearest Station = {{ill|Gakunan Railway|ja|岳南鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakunan Railway|1=Gakunan Railway Company Ltd.|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24886051}} {{ill|Yoshiwara-honchō Station|en|Yoshiwara-honchō Station|WD=Q8056327|lt=Yoshiwara Honmachi Station|1=Yoshiwara-honchō Station}} | Direct Bus = | Parking = | URL = http://www.yoshiwara.net/gion/ | Notes = }}
'''Yoshiwara Gion Festival''' (吉原祇園祭, Yoshiwara Gion-sai) is a festival held annually on the second Saturday and Sunday of June in the Yoshiwara district of {{ill|Fuji, Shizuoka|en|Fuji, Shizuoka|WD=Q328613|lt=Fuji, Shizuoka|1=Fuji, Shizuoka}}, formerly {{ill|Yoshiwara, Shizuoka|en|Yoshiwara, Shizuoka|WD=Q997390|lt=Yoshiwara City|1=Yoshiwara, Shizuoka}}, Shizuoka Prefecture.
== Overview == * One of the historically long-standing festivals originating during the time of Yoshiwara City. * Known locally as "Oten no San," it is called the foremost Gion Festival in the Tōkai region. * The primary venue, {{ill|Yoshiwara Shopping Street|ja|吉原商店街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Shopping Street|1=Yoshiwara Shopping Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133934514}}, stretches approximately 1 km with over 200 stalls lining the streets, attracting more than 200,000 visitors over two days. * Although named a {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en|Gion Matsuri|WD=Q979873|lt=Gion Festival|1=Gion Matsuri}}, it is not a direct continuation of Kyoto's Gion Festival. The style of the festival reflects influences from various local traditions, as Yoshiwara was a {{ill|Shukuba|lt=Shukuba|lt=Post town|ja|宿場町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/post town|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宿場}}.
== Venue == * Centered around {{ill|Yoshiwara Shopping Street|ja|吉原商店街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Shopping Street|1=Yoshiwara Shopping Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133934514}} in the former Yoshiwara city center (now the central area of Fuji City). * From 13:00 to 21:00 during the festival, the shopping street is closed to vehicular traffic. * Access: ** Train: Alight at {{ill|Yoshiwara-honchō Station|en|Yoshiwara-honchō Station|WD=Q8056327|lt=Yoshiwara Honmachi Station|1=Yoshiwara-honchō Station}} on the {{ill|Gakunan Railway Line|ja|岳南電車岳南線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakunan Railway Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岳南電車岳南鉄道線|1=Gakunan Railway Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5517715}}. ** Bus: Alight at {{ill|Yoshiwara Chūō Station|ja|吉原中央駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Chūō Station|1=Q11412690|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412690}} on {{ill|Fujikyu Shizuoka Bus|ja|富士急静岡バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujikyu Shizuoka Bus|1=Fujikyu Shizuoka Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11456063}}.
== Floats == [[File:Dashi.jpg|thumb|300px|Parading floats]] * Reflecting the cultural crossroads of the Tōkaidō, the festival features three types of floats: "traditional float," "stage float," and a hybrid combining elements of both. These 21 unique floats parade through the town. * The tradition reportedly began in the third year of Manji (1660) when a shopkeeper, having prayed to the deity {{ill|Tenjin|en|Tenjin|WD=Q1305727|lt=Tenjin|1=Tenjin}}, fulfilled a vow by donating a shrine. During the year's Tenjin festival, residents mounted wheels on boats and pulled them in a procession.
== Mikoshi (Portable Shrine) == * The {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=Mikoshi|1=Mikoshi}}, representing {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオノミコト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, is known for its fierce and dynamic style, earning the nickname "Kenka Mikoshi" (Fighting Shrine). * In the past, the mikoshi was handled so violently that it occasionally damaged houses. Locals refer to carrying the mikoshi as "shaking" rather than carrying, reflecting its vigorous movement. * Bamboo leaves attached to the mikoshi are considered talismans for warding off evil when displayed at home.<ref group="Note">This belief originates from the {{ill|Somin Shōrai legend|ja|蘇民将来伝説|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Somin Shōrai legend|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which says Susanoo will protect homes marked with bamboo leaves.</ref>
== Taiko Drumming == [[File:Miyadaiko.jpg|thumb|300px|Shrine Drums]] * Performed on {{ill|Nagadō Taiko|ja|長胴太鼓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagadō Taiko|1=nagadō daiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653585}} by two drummers, originally used to alert the community to the mikoshi's arrival. Known as "Call Drums" or "Fure Drums." * On Saturday evening, representatives from various neighborhoods compete in performances across Yoshiwara Shopping Street. * On Sunday, the drums fulfill their traditional role as "Call Drums" preceding the mikoshi.
== Oten no San Stamp Rally == * Visitors collect stamps at neighborhood venues marked in the festival guide. Collecting at least 10 stamps qualifies for a prize draw at Lacrosse Yoshiwara 2F Community F. * Stamp collection: Sunday of the festival, 13:00–15:00. * Prize draw: Lacrosse Yoshiwara 2F, Sunday, 13:00–15:00.
== Additional Notes == * Due to the {{ill|Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|en|Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|WD=Q1136168|lt=2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|1=Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami}}, the festival in 2011 was limited to mikoshi rituals, with taiko performances and food stalls canceled. * In 2023, to prevent the involvement of {{ill|yakuza|ja|暴力団|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yakuza|1=Japanese organized crime group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11090465}}, stall owners and workers were required to display photo IDs.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/20230611-OYT1T50131/2/ |title=Preventing Organized Crime at Festivals: Yomiuri Shimbun |publisher=Yomiuri Shimbun |date=June 11, 2023 |accessdate=June 12, 2023}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == See Also == * {{ill|Yoshiwara-juku|en|Yoshiwara-juku|WD=Q3069833|lt=Yoshiwara-juku|1=Yoshiwara-juku}} * {{ill|Yoshiwara Shopping Street|ja|吉原商店街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Shopping Street|1=Yoshiwara Shopping Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133934514}} * {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en|Gion Matsuri|WD=Q979873|lt=Gion Festival|1=Gion Matsuri}}
